div.p: This case-sensitive code system http://hl7.org/fhir/us/codex-ocpa/CodeSystem/ocpa-codes defines the following codes:

tr.td: An Observation that documents the ordinal line of systemic anti-cancer therapy a patient is receiving or has received. Migration candidate for LOINC.
A regimen component that is an anti-cancer therapeutic agent (e.g., cytotoxic, targeted, immunotherapy).
A regimen component that is supportive (e.g., antiemetic, growth factor, steroid not used as anti-cancer therapy).
A regimen component administered as premedication prior to the primary agent.

description.value: Local codes defined by the OGCA IG for concepts that do not yet have an established representation in LOINC, SNOMED CT, or mCODE. These codes are migration candidates and should be retired in favor of standard codes as they become available.

purpose.value: mCODE Migration Candidate — individual codes submitted to LOINC and SNOMED CT. This code system will be retired once standard codes are assigned. It is NOT intended to be a permanent artifact of this IG. See the mCODE Candidates page for the migration plan.

definition.value: An Observation that documents the ordinal line of systemic anti-cancer therapy a patient is receiving or has received. Migration candidate for LOINC.
A regimen component that is an anti-cancer therapeutic agent (e.g., cytotoxic, targeted, immunotherapy).
A regimen component that is supportive (e.g., antiemetic, growth factor, steroid not used as anti-cancer therapy).
A regimen component administered as premedication prior to the primary agent.
